_meta.name: English (Canada)
_meta.review_note: All strings reviewed by tech-writer and labour lawyer before any production launch (plan §13 item E). OHSA / s.50 / s.43 / s.51 wording in particular.
_meta.placeholders: Variables use {name} syntax; integers/dates locale-format at render time.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Try again, or contact your worker co-chair if the problem continues.
errors.network: Cannot reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
errors.permission: You don't have permission to do this. If you think you should, contact your worker co-chair.
errors.session_expired: Your session has ended. Sign in again to continue.
errors.rate_limit: You've done this too many times. Try again in {minutes} minutes.
errors.crypto: Could not decrypt the data on this device. Try locking and signing in again. If this keeps happening, your device may have been tampered with — contact your worker co-chair.
errors.integrity_fail: The data on this device could not be verified. This entry may have been tampered with and was not saved. Please re-enter it.

filterEmptyState.no_matches: No matches for this filter. Pick a different chip above, or clear the filter.

csvDownload.helper: Downloads a CSV of the rows currently shown (filter-aware).

shareUrl.error_announce: Could not copy the share URL. Please copy it from the address bar.

print.generated_at: Generated {stamp} from the JHSC committee app.

keyboardShortcuts.heading: Keyboard shortcuts
keyboardShortcuts.share_hint: Look for the “Copy share URL” button on register surfaces.

savedViews.name_aria: Name the view you want to save

savedViewsPage.title: Saved views
savedViewsPage.heading: Saved views
savedViewsPage.intro: Filtered register views you've bookmarked on this device. Click a name to jump back into that view, rename to relabel, or delete to remove.
savedViewsPage.empty: No saved views yet. From any register, narrow the filters you want and click "Save view".
savedViewsPage.filter_aria: Filter saved views by name or route
savedViewsPage.rename_aria: New name for this saved view
savedViewsPage.import_error: Couldn't read that file. Make sure it's a saved-views export.

helpPage.title: Help
helpPage.heading: Using the committee app
helpPage.intro: Every register surface is URL-driven — the chips, sort, and date range collapse into a shareable URL. Here are the shortcuts and conventions that make day-to-day work faster.
helpPage.urls_body: After narrowing a register with chips, sort, or a date range, hit “Copy share URL” to put the current URL on your clipboard — paste it into the minutes or a peer chat to send the exact same view.
helpPage.csv_body: “Download CSV” exports only the rows currently shown (the same rows the active filter and sort produce). Use this for committee meetings and employer follow-ups.
helpPage.report_body: /report rolls up activity across every register for a chosen month or year. Each total tile deep-links into the matching register. Use the print stylesheet for paper handouts.
helpPage.saved_views_body: Click “Save view” on any register to bookmark its filtered URL under a name. Tick “Pin to home” to promote that view to the dashboard. Manage every saved view at /saved-views — rename, delete, pin/unpin, or export the whole set as JSON for another device.
helpPage.recent_searches_body: The header search keeps your last five queries on this device. Focus the input to surface them as a dropdown and re-run with one click; the × removes a single entry. Searches are stored locally — no committee data leaves your device.

errorPage.title: Something went wrong
errorPage.body_404: The page you were looking for doesn't exist. It may have moved, or the link may be wrong.
errorPage.body_other: An unexpected error occurred. Try the action again, or return to the home page.

privacyPage.title: Privacy
privacyPage.heading: Privacy
privacyPage.placeholder_body: The full privacy policy is being finalized. In the meantime, if you have questions about how this app handles your information, contact your worker co-chair.
privacyPage.summary_heading: What the system can and cannot see
privacyPage.summary_intro: The JHSC PWA is built so the app operator cannot read your concerns, reprisal entries, or sensitive narratives — those are encrypted on your device before they leave it. The summary below is the short version of the published policy.
privacyPage.bullet_no_third_party: No third-party JavaScript or analytics. No cookies are used to track you across sites.
privacyPage.bullet_local_first: Your concerns, recovery passphrase, and identity keys live on your device. The server stores only the encrypted blobs.
privacyPage.bullet_audit: An append-only audit log records every action you take (e.g. 'opened a concern', 'wiped this device') using a pseudonym derived from your worker ID — not your name.
privacyPage.bullet_no_pi_logs: Crash and error logs are scrubbed of personally identifying information before they are sent to the operator.

concernsPage.title: Concerns
concernsPage.heading: Concerns intake
concernsPage.coming_soon_body: The concerns intake surface is being wired to the backend. When it ships, you'll be able to file an anonymous concern by default, choose to identify yourself on a per-concern basis, and pick the hazard class and severity. Every concern is encrypted on this device before it leaves; only authorized worker members can decrypt it.
concernsPage.bullet_anonymous_default: Anonymous by default — the toggle starts ON every time. You can choose to identify yourself, and that choice does not persist across concerns.
concernsPage.bullet_encrypted: Encrypted on this device — the narrative leaves your device as ciphertext. The server cannot read it.
concernsPage.bullet_audit_pseudonym: Audit-logged with a pseudonym — the action of filing the concern is logged, but the audit row never carries your name.
concernsPage.bullet_no_employer_disclosure: Never sent to the employer side — concerns stay within the worker side of the committee until a deliberate export decision.

reprisalPage.title: Reprisal log
reprisalPage.heading: Reprisal log
reprisalPage.coming_soon_body: The reprisal-log intake surface is being wired to the backend. When it ships, you'll be able to record a suspected reprisal incident under a four-bullet OHSA consent contract. Reprisal entries are sensitivity C4 — the highest tier — and remain encrypted at rest with per-entry passphrases.
reprisalPage.bullet_consent_per_intake: A fresh consent contract renders on every intake — the four-bullet acknowledgment does not 'stick' across sessions.
reprisalPage.bullet_per_entry_passphrase: A per-entry passphrase encrypts the entry; without that passphrase, even authorized members cannot read it back.
reprisalPage.bullet_actor_visible_to_author: Your authorship is visible to you in your own UI; the pseudonymized projection hides the author from other members' feeds.
reprisalPage.bullet_ohsa_reminder: OHSA section 50 prohibits reprisal against workers who exercise rights under the Act — the consent text on the intake surface restates this in plain language.

inspectionsPage.title: Inspections
inspectionsPage.heading: Workplace inspections
inspectionsPage.coming_soon_body: The monthly-inspection capture surface is being wired to the backend. When it ships, the JHSC will use it to record the at-least-monthly workplace inspection required by OHSA section 9(26)(b), with offline-first capture for shop-floor walks beyond cell signal.
inspectionsPage.bullet_ohsa_monthly: Monthly inspections per OHSA s. 9(26)(b) — the JHSC walks the workplace at least once a month and records what was checked.
inspectionsPage.bullet_offline_first: Offline-first capture — you can record an inspection in a basement, on a roof, or anywhere without cell signal; entries sync when you're back online.
inspectionsPage.bullet_integrity_tag: Per-entry HMAC integrity tag — every inspection carries a cryptographic seal so server-side tampering is detectable.
inspectionsPage.bullet_photo_sanitize: Photos are sanitized before encryption — EXIF metadata (GPS, timestamps, device fingerprints) is stripped on this device first.

minutesPage.title: Minutes
minutesPage.heading: Meeting minutes
minutesPage.coming_soon_body: The meeting-minutes drafting surface is being wired to the backend. When it ships, the JHSC will use it to capture committee deliberations, hold drafts in worker-side encryption until approval, and publish finalized minutes only with a quorum present.
minutesPage.bullet_worker_side_drafts: Worker-side deliberation — drafts are encrypted on this device and stay readable only by authorized worker members until the committee approves them.
minutesPage.bullet_approval_gated: Approval-gated publication — only the worker co-chair can promote a draft to 'approved', and only with a documented quorum.
minutesPage.bullet_revision_history: Append-only revision history — every edit is captured for audit; nothing is silently overwritten.
minutesPage.bullet_quoted_consent: Quoted-concern consent — if a minute quotes a filed concern, the original author must consent before the minute can be approved.

recommendationsPage.title: Recommendations
recommendationsPage.heading: Recommendations register
recommendationsPage.coming_soon_body: The recommendations register is being wired to the backend. When it ships, the JHSC will use it to track every recommendation made to the employer, hold the 21-day response timer (OHSA s. 50(7)(c)), and surface auto-escalation flags when a recommendation goes unanswered.
recommendationsPage.bullet_21_day_timer: 21-day response timer per OHSA s. 50(7)(c) — the employer must respond in writing to a recommendation within 21 days.
recommendationsPage.bullet_auto_escalation: Auto-escalation on expiry — a recommendation that times out without a written response is flagged for the next meeting and recorded in the audit log.
recommendationsPage.bullet_traceability: Linked to concerns and inspections — every recommendation traces back to the safety issue that prompted it.
recommendationsPage.bullet_export_deliberate: Export is deliberate — a recommendation reaches the employer co-chair only via the four-eyes export flow, never silently or automatically.

workRefusalPage.title: Work refusal (s.43)
workRefusalPage.heading: Work refusal (OHSA s. 43)
workRefusalPage.coming_soon_body: The work-refusal capture surface is being wired to the backend. When it ships, a worker can record a refusal to perform work they believe is unsafe under OHSA section 43, with stage-gated documentation through the s. 43(4) investigation and (if needed) Ministry of Labour escalation under s. 43(8).
workRefusalPage.bullet_right_to_refuse: Right to refuse unsafe work — OHSA s. 43 protects a worker who refuses work they have reason to believe is dangerous; the surface captures the refusal in worker-side encryption.
workRefusalPage.bullet_stage_gated: Stage-gated documentation — the worker, then the joint investigation under s. 43(4), then (if unresolved) Ministry escalation under s. 43(8). Each stage carries its own entry.
workRefusalPage.bullet_certified_member_visibility: Worker certified member visibility — entries are readable by the worker certified member; never the employer co-chair without a deliberate export.
workRefusalPage.bullet_audit_full_chain: Audit-log full chain — every action (refusal filed, employer notified, investigation outcome, escalation) is logged with pseudonymized actor IDs.

s51Page.title: Critical injury (s.51)
s51Page.heading: Critical injury (OHSA s. 51)
s51Page.coming_soon_body: The s.51 critical-injury evidence surface is being wired to the backend. When it ships, the worker member required to be present at the s. 51 investigation will use it to capture evidence — photos, witness statements, scene notes — in sensitivity-C4 encryption, with the 48-hour scene-preservation timer surfaced as a deadline.
s51Page.bullet_worker_member_present: Worker member present — OHSA s. 51 requires a worker member of the JHSC to be present at the investigation of any critical injury or fatality; this surface captures what that member sees.
s51Page.bullet_scene_preservation: Scene preservation — the s. 51(2) 48-hour preservation requirement is surfaced as a countdown so the evidence window is visible to the team.
s51Page.bullet_c4_encryption: Sensitivity C4 — evidence is encrypted at the highest tier with per-entry passphrases; ciphertext leaves the device, plaintext never does.
s51Page.bullet_photo_sanitize: Photos are sanitized — EXIF metadata (GPS, timestamps, device fingerprints) is stripped on this device before encryption.

libraryPage.title: Document library
libraryPage.heading: Committee document library
libraryPage.coming_soon_body: The document library is being wired up. When it ships, the JHSC will have a shared, version-controlled space for OHSA references, the workplace's safety policies, training materials, and any documents committee members need at meetings or on the shop floor.
libraryPage.bullet_versioned: Versioned — every document carries its revision; superseded versions stay accessible so a worker can quote a policy as it was at the time of an incident.
libraryPage.bullet_offline_cache: Offline cache — the most-referenced documents (OHSA Part III/IV, workplace policies, the JHSC terms of reference) are cached on the device so they're readable without cell signal.
libraryPage.bullet_search: Searchable on-device — the index lives in the browser; queries never leave your device, so 'what did I look up' is not visible to the operator.
libraryPage.bullet_committee_scoped: Committee-scoped — documents are visible to authorized worker members; the employer side cannot read what the committee references unless explicitly shared.

trainingPage.title: Training records
trainingPage.heading: Training records
trainingPage.coming_soon_body: The training-records surface is being wired up. When it ships, the JHSC will use it to track which committee members have completed which training modules — the OHSA s. 9(12)(d) baseline (the worker certified member program), refresher cycles, and any workplace-specific training the employer is required to provide.
trainingPage.bullet_certified_member: Worker certified member tracking — OHSA s. 9(12)(d) requires at least one worker member who has completed the certified-member program; the surface surfaces who holds the certification and when it expires.
trainingPage.bullet_refresher_alerts: Refresher alerts — certifications and workplace-specific training have expiry dates; the surface flags upcoming and overdue refreshers.
trainingPage.bullet_pseudonymized_audit: Pseudonymized audit — training completion events are logged with pseudonymized actor IDs, so the audit trail records who was trained without exposing names in unrelated views.
trainingPage.bullet_evidence_attach: Evidence attachments — certificates and course completion records can be attached and encrypted alongside the record.

auditPage.title: Audit log
auditPage.heading: Audit log
auditPage.coming_soon_body: The audit-log viewer is being wired to the backend. When it ships, authorized worker members will be able to scroll the append-only record of every committee action — who did what, when, with pseudonymized actor IDs — and verify the Merkle integrity chain end-to-end (T18).
auditPage.bullet_append_only: Append-only — rows are never updated or deleted; corrections land as additional rows that reference the original.
auditPage.bullet_pseudonymized: Pseudonymized actor IDs — the audit row carries an HMAC-pseudonym derived from the actor's user_id, not their name (ADR-0016).
auditPage.bullet_merkle_integrity: Merkle integrity chain — every batch of audit rows is hashed into a Merkle tree; tampering on the server is detectable client-side (T18).
auditPage.bullet_retention_aware: Retention-aware — rows age out under the ADR-0017 schedule; the viewer surfaces what remains and what was swept.

sensitiveFeedPage.title: Recent sensitive activity
sensitiveFeedPage.heading: Recent sensitive activity
sensitiveFeedPage.coming_soon_body: The sensitive-activity feed is being wired to the backend. When it ships, the worker co-chair and worker certified member will use it to monitor recent C3/C4 sensitivity activity — concerns filed, reprisal entries created, work-refusal stages advanced — without reading the underlying narratives, which remain encrypted.
sensitiveFeedPage.bullet_metadata_only: Metadata only — the feed shows the action and the pseudonymized actor; the narrative content stays encrypted and never appears in the feed.
sensitiveFeedPage.bullet_c3_c4_scoped: C3 / C4 scoped — only sensitive-tier events surface here; routine actions (training records, library reads) live in the full audit log.
sensitiveFeedPage.bullet_export_deliberate: Export is deliberate — the feed cannot be forwarded to the employer side; the four-eyes export flow is the only path off the worker side.
sensitiveFeedPage.bullet_role_gated: Role-gated — visibility is limited to the worker co-chair and the worker certified member; an ordinary member sees only their own actions.

morePage.title: More
morePage.heading: All committee surfaces
morePage.intro: Every product surface in the app, grouped by purpose. Most are placeholders today — the full intake and viewer surfaces land as the matching sibling tasks ship.
morePage.group_intake_blurb: Worker-side-encrypted capture for concerns, reprisal, work refusal, critical injury, and monthly inspections.
morePage.group_deliberation_blurb: Meeting minutes (worker-side drafts) and the recommendations register with the 21-day OHSA timer.
morePage.group_reference_blurb: Committee document library and JHSC member training records.
morePage.group_monitoring_blurb: Append-only audit log and the role-gated sensitive-activity feed.
morePage.link_reprisal_blurb: Record a suspected reprisal incident under the OHSA s. 50 consent contract.
morePage.link_work_refusal_blurb: Document a refusal to perform unsafe work and the s. 43 investigation chain.
morePage.link_s51_blurb: Capture s.51 critical-injury evidence with the 48-hour scene-preservation timer.
morePage.link_inspections_blurb: Run the monthly OHSA s. 9(26)(b) walkthrough offline-first.
morePage.link_minutes_blurb: Draft and approve committee meeting minutes.
morePage.link_recommendations_blurb: Track recommendations and the 21-day employer-response timer.
morePage.link_library_blurb: OHSA references, workplace policies, and committee documents (versioned).
morePage.link_training_blurb: JHSC member training, certified-member tracking, refresher alerts.
morePage.link_audit_blurb: Append-only record of every committee action with the Merkle integrity chain.
morePage.link_sensitive_feed_blurb: Role-gated metadata feed for C3/C4 events.
morePage.link_report_blurb: Per-month roll-up of every register, with concern and recommendation breakdowns.
morePage.link_settings_blurb: Sign out, wipe this device, theme preferences.
morePage.link_privacy_blurb: What the system can and cannot see.
morePage.link_help_blurb: Keyboard shortcuts, how URLs share filters, CSV export, and the monthly report.
morePage.link_saved_views_blurb: Bookmark filtered register views and reuse them on this device.

device.heading: Use a personal device only
device.body: If you are reading this on a phone or laptop your employer owns, pays for, or manages, your employer may be able to read what you do here. Stop. Install on a personal phone or laptop and start again.
device.continue: This is a personal device — continue
device.stop: Stop — I'll switch to a personal device
device.stop_confirm_body: You can come back any time on a personal device.

hosting.heading: Where your data lives
hosting.body: Your committee's data is stored on Supabase, a company based in the US that runs the servers in Canada (ca-central-1). Two things to know: (1) the worker-side content — concerns, reprisal entries, minutes drafts — is encrypted on your device before it leaves, so Supabase only ever sees scrambled bytes. (2) US legal process could in principle reach the company, but what they would get back is the same scrambled bytes.

browser.unsupported_body: The JHSC App needs a recent version of Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari to set up passkeys safely. Update your browser or use a different one on this device and try again.

passkey.heading: Set up your passkey
passkey.explainer: A passkey is a way to sign in without a password. It lives on this device and uses your fingerprint, face, or device PIN to confirm it's you. There is no password to leak or guess.
passkey.failed_body: Try again. If your device doesn't support passkeys, use a different personal device.

recovery.heading: Your recovery sheet
recovery.body: If you lose this device AND your passkey, the only way back into your account is the recovery passphrase below. There is no admin who can reset it. Print this sheet and store it somewhere safe — not at work.
recovery.copy_warning: Copying is not a substitute for printing. The clipboard is not secure.

show_again.label: Press and hold to show the passphrase again
show_again.helper: Anyone who can see this screen can read the passphrase. Hold the button down for a moment to reveal it; release to hide. You can do this three times per enrollment — after that, restart enrollment to see it again.
show_again.helper_capped: You've used all three reveals in this enrollment. To see the passphrase again, restart enrollment from the start of the recovery step.

verify.heading: Type the passphrase back to confirm
verify.helper: Type the words exactly as printed, separated by spaces.
verify.wrong_body: Check the printed sheet and try again. {attempts_left} attempts left.
verify.exhausted_body: We'll show the passphrase again so you can re-verify.

recovery.title: JHSC App — Recovery sheet for {display_name}
recovery.warning: If anyone has this sheet, they can recover your account on a new device. Treat it like a key. Do not store it at work.

intake.heading: Log a concern
intake.subheading: Recorded by you on behalf of a worker. Anonymous by default.

anon.helper_on: The original worker's name is not stored. Only what you type in the body is.
anon.helper_off: You are about to record a worker's name.

named.advisory_heading: About to save a worker's name
named.advisory_body: The source name will be encrypted under the committee key. Every active committee member can see it. You cannot un-name it once you save.

saved.heading: Concern logged
saved.summary_named: Saved with a named source. Visible to all active committee members.

empty.heading: No concerns logged yet
empty.body: When a worker raises something, log it here. The intake form encrypts the body on this device before saving.

list.row_named_label: Named source — tap to reveal name

viewer.heading: Concerns register
viewer.intro: Every concern the JHSC has on file. Anonymous by default; revealed only with the worker's consent.

anon_note.label: Anonymous by default
anon_note.value: Under F-17, every concern is filed anonymously by default. The source identity stays hidden behind the actor pseudonym unless the worker who filed it consents to being named.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real concerns backend (intake form + Supabase client + committee-key decryption + audit emission) ships with T08.1.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the concerns register. Try again.

empty.heading: No reprisal entries logged
empty.body: A reprisal entry records suspected retaliation against a worker for raising a health-and-safety concern. OHSA s.50 protects workers from this. Entries are the highest sensitivity tier in this app.

create.heading: Log a reprisal entry
create.subheading: Highest sensitivity. Your name will be recorded as the author. The body is encrypted on this device.

field.title: Title
field.passphrase: Set a passphrase for this entry

passphrase.helper: This passphrase is a friction layer to view the entry later. The actual encryption uses your passkey-derived keys.

consent.heading: Before you log a reprisal
consent.lead: A reprisal entry is the most sensitive record this app holds. Here is exactly what happens when you submit it.
consent.bullet_encrypted: What is sealed and only readable by the committee: the entry body, any names, dates, witnesses, and any details you write or attach. These are locked to the committee key and never readable by the employer, the hosting provider, or anyone outside the committee.
consent.bullet_visible: What other active committee members will see in the recent-activity feed: that a reprisal entry exists, when it was created (to the nearest hour), and the reprisal entry's ID. They will NOT see who created it. Forensic review can identify the author only after a two-member committee approval, the same way reprisal entries are deleted.
consent.bullet_why: Why other members see the entry exists: to deter anyone — including a co-opted committee member — from quietly logging or reading reprisal entries on someone else's behalf. The committee acts as a check on itself.
consent.bullet_not_visible: What other members will NOT see: your name, your pseudonym, the content of the entry, or any worker named in the entry.
consent.ohsa_reminder: OHSA s.50 reminder: reprisal against you for using this app is itself an OHSA offence. The committee can pursue a s.50 complaint to the Ontario Labour Relations Board.
consent.checkbox_label: I understand what other committee members will see, and I want to submit this entry.

passphrase.heading: Enter the passphrase for this entry
passphrase.label: Passphrase
passphrase.helper: This is a friction layer. The entry is also protected by your passkey-derived keys.
passphrase.exhausted_body: This attempt has been logged. Wait before trying again.

read.your_read_announce: Your read of this reprisal entry was logged at {timestamp} and other members will be notified.

fourEyes.heading: This change needs a second committee member's approval
fourEyes.body: Status changes on reprisal entries require two active committee members. Propose the change; another member will see it in their sensitive-activity feed and approve.
fourEyes.self_approve_denied_heading: You can't approve your own proposal
fourEyes.self_approve_denied_body: Another active member must approve. This is by design.

viewer.heading: Reprisal log register
viewer.intro: Suspected reprisal incidents the JHSC has on file. OHSA s.50 protects workers who exercise rights under the Act.

c4_note.label: Highest sensitivity tier (C4)
c4_note.value: Every entry is encrypted at rest. The narrative body is sealed under a per-entry passphrase by default; without that passphrase, even authorized members cannot read it back. Source identity stays hidden behind the actor pseudonym unless the author has consented to being named.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real backend (per-entry passphrase derivation + audit emission + role-gated read path + four-eyes status changes) ships with T13.1.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the reprisal log. Try again.

page.title: Recent sensitive activity
page.subtitle: Reads of reprisal entries, exports to the employer co-chair, and other sensitive events from across the committee.

empty.heading: No recent sensitive activity
empty.body: When another member reads a reprisal entry or exports anything to the employer co-chair, you will see it here.

muted.banner: Notification pop-ups paused. The feed still updates. Audit logging is unaffected.

error.load_failed: Unable to load recent activity. Retry.

reauth.heading: Confirm with your passkey to continue
reauth.body: Exports leave the worker side. Your current session is not enough — you need to confirm with your passkey now.
reauth.failed_body: Try again, or cancel and start over.

fields.heading: These fields will leave the worker side
fields.subheading: This is the entire allowlist. Anything not listed here is not included.

recipient.label: Recipient: Employer co-chair (manual delivery — you will send the file yourself).

concern_flag.heading: This export includes items derived from worker concerns
concern_flag.body: Some items being exported came from worker concerns logged on the worker side. Review the originating concerns below before confirming.
concern_flag.confirm_checkbox: I have reviewed the concern-derived items.

confirm.disabled_hint: Tick the confirmation box above to enable.

exported.heading: Export ready
exported.notified_label: Other members will be notified within 60 seconds.

empty.heading: Nothing to export yet
empty.body: There are no finalized items to export. Finalize the document first.

error.integrity_body: The list of fields about to be exported does not match the renderer. The export was cancelled and an audit row was written. Try again — if this keeps happening, contact the worker co-chair.

capture.heading: Photo capture
capture.advisory_inline: GPS removed automatically. Enter location as text or pick from the list.

preview.heading: Review before attaching
preview.gps_advisory: All location and metadata has been removed from this photo. The location below — if you enter it — is what will be saved.

location.label: Location (optional, free text)
location.select_label: Or pick from the location list
location.placeholder: e.g. South dock, line 3, near the hydraulic press

status.upload_failed: Upload failed — will retry on next sync
status.integrity_failed_body: This entry could not be verified and was not saved. A malicious extension may have modified the queue. Please re-take and re-enter.

partial.heading: {succeeded} synced, {failed} failed verification
partial.body: These entries could not be verified and were not saved. Open the list to re-enter them.

lock.heading: Locked — confirm it's you to continue
lock.body: Your committee data is protected and was cleared from memory.

panic.heading: Wipe this device's data
panic.body: This will remove from this device: the encrypted local store, your current session, and cached settings. It does NOT remove data from the server — other devices and the committee still have their access. You cannot undo this on this device.
panic.complete_body: Sign in again to continue, or close this tab to leave the app.

page.title: Sessions on this account
page.subtitle: Other devices where you are signed in. Revoke any that you don't recognize.

revoke_all.confirm_body: All sessions other than this device will be signed out. This is immediate.

current_revoke.confirm_body: You will be signed out of this device. You can sign back in with your passkey.

signIn.title: Sign in
signIn.intro: Use the passkey on this device to sign in to JHSC.

reason.bad_request: Couldn't process the sign-in request. Try again.
reason.assertion_invalid: Your passkey couldn't be verified. Try again, and if the problem continues, set up this device from the home page.
reason.unknown_credential: This passkey isn't recognized on this server. If this device is new, choose "Start setup" from the home page instead.
reason.mint_failed: We couldn't establish your session right now. Try again in a moment.
reason.unknown: Something went wrong. Try again, or contact your worker co-chair if the problem continues.

signIn.already_signed_in: You're already signed in on this device.

landing.subtitle: Health and safety, for the people doing the work.

new_device.heading: New device
new_device.description: Set up this device for the first time. You'll enrol a passkey and store a recovery passphrase.

returning_device.heading: Already enrolled
returning_device.description: Sign in with the passkey on this device.

signed_in.heading: Welcome back
signed_in.description: You're already signed in on this device.

dashboard.heading: Needs attention
dashboard.intro: A snapshot of open work across the registers. Tap any tile to dive in.

recent.heading: Recent activity
recent.intro: The latest entries in the worker-side audit log. Pseudonymized actors; no PI.
recent.empty_filtered: No recent activity in this category.

page.title: Search
page.heading: Search across the registers
page.intro: Find concerns, recommendations, inspections, minutes, training records, and more. Searches titles, areas, event types, and short previews.
page.label: Search query
page.helper: Case-insensitive substring match. Pseudonymized actors only; no PI.
page.empty_state: Type a query above to search across the registers.
page.no_results: No matches for {query}. Try a different word or check spelling.

signOut.heading: Session
signOut.intro: Sign out on this device. The server-side session expires within a few minutes; for immediate device cleanup, use Wipe this device's data below.
signOut.signed_out: Signed out. You can sign back in with your passkey.

device_data.heading: Device data
device_data.intro_before_emphasis: Erase the encrypted local store, your current sign-in, and queued entries on this device. This does
device_data.intro_after_emphasis: sign you out on the server or other devices.

sessions.heading: Active sessions
sessions.intro: Every device that is currently signed in to your account. Revoke a session to sign that device out immediately.
sessions.empty: You have no other active sessions.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load your sessions. Try again, or contact your worker co-chair if the problem continues.
error.revoke_failed: That session couldn't be revoked. It may have already expired — try refreshing.
error.revoke_all_failed: Couldn't revoke the other sessions. Try again in a moment.
error.signed_out: You're not signed in. Sign in to see your active sessions.

deviceInfo.heading: This device
deviceInfo.intro: What the app is running on. Useful when you're troubleshooting with your worker co-chair — none of this information leaves your device.

sentry_test.helper: Sends a no-content test event so the worker co-chair can confirm error reporting works. No personal information is included.

recoveryVerify.heading: Verify your recovery sheet
recoveryVerify.intro: Upload the JSON file from your recovery sheet and type the passphrase to check that the sheet still works. This is a periodic check-in — it runs entirely on this device, nothing is sent to the server, and no key material is saved.
recoveryVerify.passphrase_helper: Type the words exactly as printed on the sheet, separated by spaces.

success.heading: Your recovery sheet works.
success.body: The JSON file and the passphrase decrypted successfully. Store this sheet somewhere safe — not at work.

error.not_json: That file is not valid JSON. Check that you uploaded the recovery sheet that the app gave you.
error.wrong_shape: That JSON does not look like a recovery sheet. The expected file has four top-level fields (ciphertext, kdf_params, version, blob_id).
error.wrong_version: This recovery sheet uses a version this app doesn't support. Contact your worker co-chair.
error.bad_base64: Part of the recovery sheet is corrupted. Check whether the file was modified after it was downloaded.
error.bad_nonce_length: The recovery sheet is too short to be valid. Re-download it from the original device if possible.
error.decrypt_failed: The recovery sheet did not decrypt with that passphrase. Re-check the passphrase letter-by-letter, or use a fresh recovery sheet.
error.argon2_unavailable: Your browser cannot run the Argon2id crypto needed to verify this sheet. Try a different browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari, recent versions).
error.file_read: Could not read the file. Try selecting it again.
error.unknown: Something went wrong verifying the recovery sheet. Try again, or contact your worker co-chair if the problem continues.

about.heading: About this app
about.intro: Version, security disclosure, and links to the public documents that describe what the app does and doesn't do.
about.threat_model_link: Read the threat model on GitHub
about.decisions_link: Read the decisions document on GitHub
about.license_note: Not legal advice. A privacy lawyer and a labour-law lawyer must review the app before any production launch.

recoveryReissue.heading: Print a new recovery sheet
recoveryReissue.intro: Generate a fresh recovery sheet (new JSON file + new passphrase) for the identity that's already on this device. Useful if the printed sheet is lost, smudged, or you want to rotate the passphrase.

warning.old_sheet_still_works: Your OLD paper sheet still works with the OLD passphrase — destroy the old print after you've stored the new one. The system cannot enforce paper destruction.
warning.server_backup_unchanged: This updates the sheet ON THIS DEVICE only. The server-stored backup is unchanged and still requires the original passphrase for the lost-device recovery flow.

success.heading: New recovery sheet issued.
success.body: The JSON file downloaded to this device. Write the passphrase below on the print-out you store with it.

error.signed_out: You're not signed in. Sign in to print a new recovery sheet.
error.no_identity: Couldn't find an identity key on this device. Sign in or re-onboard.
error.download_failed: The JSON file couldn't be downloaded. Check that pop-ups are allowed and try again.
error.unknown: Something went wrong generating the recovery sheet. Try again, or contact your worker co-chair if the problem continues.

viewer.heading: Meeting minutes register
viewer.intro: Every set of meeting minutes the JHSC has drafted, approved, or archived. Drafts stay encrypted on the worker side; approval requires a documented quorum.

approval_note.label: Approval gate
approval_note.value: Only the worker co-chair can promote a draft to approved, and only with a documented quorum. Minutes that quote a filed concern require the original author's consent before approval.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real minutes-module backend (drafts in worker-side encryption + four-eyes approval ceremony + append-only revision history + quoted-concern consent gate) ships with the minutes-module wire-up.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the minutes register. Try again.

viewer.heading: Recommendations register
viewer.intro: Every recommendation the JHSC has made to the employer, with the 21-day OHSA s. 50(7)(c) response timer.

timer.label: 21-day response timer
timer.value: Under OHSA s. 50(7)(c) the employer must respond in writing within 21 days. Recommendations that pass that window are auto-escalated to the next meeting.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real backend (recommendations store + 21-day timer + employer-response capture + auto-escalation) ships with T12.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the recommendations. Try again.

viewer.heading: Recent sensitive activity
viewer.intro: Metadata for recent C3 and C4 sensitivity-tier events. Narratives stay encrypted; this surface shows only what action was taken, by whom (pseudonymized), and when.

role.label: Who can see this
role.value: Visibility is limited to the worker co-chair and the worker certified member. An ordinary worker member sees only their own actions in this feed.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real backend (server-side aggregation + role-gating + Merkle proofs) ships with T18 / the sensitive-feed wire-up.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the sensitive-activity feed. Try again.

page.title: Audit log
page.subtitle: Every action on the worker side. Hash-chained and tamper-evident.

viewer.heading: Audit log
viewer.intro: Every action recorded on the worker side. Append-only; pseudonymized actor IDs; Merkle integrity chain when the T18 backend lands.

integrity.label: Integrity
integrity.value_demo: Demo data — the real Merkle integrity chain ships with T18.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real audit-op Edge Function + Merkle integrity chain ship with T18.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the audit log. Try again.

integrity.failed_body: The audit log appears to have been tampered with. Contact the worker co-chair immediately.
integrity.never_body: The first daily integrity check will run shortly.

action.reprisal.delete.propose: Proposed deletion of a reprisal entry
action.reprisal.delete.approve: Approved deletion of a reprisal entry
action.sensitive.access_attempt: Attempted to view a sensitive entry
action.export.minutes: Exported finalized minutes to employer co-chair
action.export.recommendation: Exported a recommendation to employer co-chair
action.export.integrity_fail: Export integrity check failed and was cancelled
action.identity_keypair.created: Created the keys this device uses to sign in
action.committee_data_key.wrapped_for_member: Granted a member access to the committee key
action.committee_data_key.unwrap: Unlocked the committee's shared data for this session
action.client.identity_selftest_fail: Identity-key self-test failed on a device
action.client.cache_policy_violation: Blocked a stale response from being shown — possible tampering
action.queue.integrity_fail: An offline-queued entry failed verification and was rejected

empty.heading: No inspections yet
empty.body: Monthly workplace inspections are an OHSA s.9(26) duty. Start a new one when you walk the floor.

viewer.heading: Inspections register
viewer.intro: Every workplace inspection the JHSC has logged. Required at least monthly under OHSA s. 9(26)(b).

offline_note.label: Offline-first capture
offline_note.value: Inspections are recorded on this device first. Each entry carries a per-entry HMAC integrity tag so server-side tampering between this device and the register is detectable on drain.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real backend (IndexedDB-backed store + PhotoCaptureSurface UI + ServiceWorker integration + real-canvas EXIF re-encode) ships with T10.1.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the inspections register. Try again.

concern_flag.detected: This export includes items derived from worker concerns. Review carefully.

export.complete: Export ready. PDF is downloaded. Audit row {audit_id} recorded.
export.post_export_notification: Other committee members are being notified of this export.

read.logged: Your read of this reprisal entry was logged at {timestamp} and other members will be notified.

fourEyes.proposed: Change proposed. Awaiting approval from another committee member.

concern.anonymous_on: Anonymous source, on. Press to switch off and record a name.
concern.anonymous_off: Named source, on. Press to switch back to anonymous.
concern.advisory_named: About to save a worker's name. The name will be encrypted but every active committee member can see it.

lock.engaged: Application locked. Sign in again to continue.

panic.complete: Local data wiped from this device.

audit.integrity_fail: Audit log integrity check failed. Contact the worker co-chair.

photo.captured: Photo captured. Review and add a location if you want one.
photo.integrity_fail: A photo could not be verified and was rejected. Please re-take it.

sensitive_feed.muted: Sensitive-activity pop-ups paused. The feed still updates.

page.title: Document library
page.subtitle: Quick reference: OHSA, regulations, committee terms of reference.

empty.heading: No documents yet
empty.body: Reference documents will appear here once they are published.

viewer.heading: Document library
viewer.intro: Versioned committee reference documents — policies, procedures, training material, legislation, and templates.

offline_note.label: Readable offline
offline_note.value: Cached documents stay readable on the shop floor beyond cell signal. Versions are append-only — superseded versions stay retrievable, nothing is silently overwritten.

viewer.empty: No documents in the library yet.
viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real library backend (versioned document store + offline cache + on-device search) ships with the library-module wire-up.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the document library. Try again.

viewer.heading: Work refusal register
viewer.intro: Work refusals under OHSA s. 43 — a worker may refuse work they have reason to believe is dangerous. Each entry tracks the staged process to resolution.

stages_note.label: Staged process
stages_note.value: A refusal moves through up to three stages: the worker's refusal (s. 43(3)), the joint investigation with the employer and a worker member (s. 43(4)), and — only if unresolved — a Ministry of Labour inspector (s. 43(8)). Most refusals resolve at the first or second stage.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real backend (stage-gated capture + worker-side encryption + audit chain) ships with the work-refusal-module wire-up.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the work-refusal register. Try again.

viewer.heading: Critical-injury evidence (s. 51)
viewer.intro: Evidence bundles for critical injuries and fatalities. OHSA s. 51 requires a worker member of the JHSC be present at the investigation.

scene_note.label: Scene preservation (s. 51(2))
scene_note.value: After a critical injury, the scene must not be disturbed until an inspector releases it or 48 hours pass. Active bundles show the hours remaining on that window. Every bundle is sensitivity C4 — sealed under a per-entry passphrase.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real backend (evidence-capture flow + scene-preservation timer + per-entry passphrase + photo sanitize) ships with T14.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the evidence register. Try again.

viewer.heading: Training records
viewer.intro: Certification tracking for committee members — OHSA s. 9(12)(d) certified-member training plus the refresher backlog.

refresher_note.label: Refresher alerts
refresher_note.value: Records turn amber 60 days before a certification expires and red once it lapses, so the refresher backlog reads at a glance. Members appear by pseudonym only.

viewer.demo_note: This is synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real backend (certified-member tracking + refresher alerts + evidence attachments) ships with the training-module wire-up.

error.load_failed: Couldn't load the training records. Try again.

page.title: Monthly report
page.heading: Monthly committee report
page.intro: Per-month roll-up of activity across every register, with concern and recommendation breakdowns. Use the month nav to compare against earlier periods.
page.demo_note: Totals are computed from synthetic demo data so you can see what the surface will look like. The real backend (cross-register query + role-aware row scoping) ships with the reporting-module wire-up.
